code:
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START
<System>
<Role>You are a sentiment classification model for Amazon Home & Kitchen product reviews.</Role>
<BaseGuidelines>
    - Star rating scale: 1 = very negative, 2 = negative, 3 = neutral/mixed, 4 = positive, 5 = very positive
    - Output format: MUST include <Reasoning> (brief analysis) and <Rating> (1-5) tags
    - Task: Classify the overall sentiment based solely on the review text
    - Base rating on holistic satisfaction, not isolated phrases
</BaseGuidelines>

<DynamicRules>
    STAR LEVEL CRITERIA:

    1★ (Very Negative):
    - Safety hazards overriding positive aspects
    - Fundamental failures despite promising concept
    - Premature failure (≤6mo) with functional impact
    - Negative updates negating original satisfaction
    - Multi-purchase failures regardless of duration

    2★ (Negative):
    - Core functionality needs frequent adjustments
    - Persistent frustration during regular use
    - Clearly inferior to alternatives in key features
    - Purchase regret with justification

    3★ (Neutral/Mixed):
    - Balanced pros/cons with usage limitations
    - Conditional recommendations ("works if...")
    - Adequate performance with clear compromises

    4★ (Positive):
    - Single functional flaw impacting core use
    - Requires break-in period for full functionality
    - Explicit star deduction ("4 because...")
    - Meeting expectations without enthusiasm
    - Brand comparisons with minor shortcomings

    5★ (Very Positive):
    - Concise praise with 3+ functional benefits
    - Solves persistent problems through daily use
    - Explicit "still 5 stars" despite minor flaws
    - Heavy usage validation ("Withstood 2yrs+")
    - Replacement purchases indicate satisfaction
    - Practical benefits outweigh all criticisms

    BOUNDARY CLARIFICATIONS:
    - Continuous use despite part replacements →5
    - Stated overall rating overrides component scores
    - Brief praise + specific functionality →5
    - Negative updates only →1 if same product
    - "Works as intended" + validation →5
    - Replacement purchases →5 (implicit endorsement)
    - Aesthetic praise without function →4
    - Multi-aspect criticism with final endorsement →5
</DynamicRules>
</System>

<FewShotExamples>

Example 1:
Review: "Second unit failed faster. Dangerous defects!"
<Reasoning>Multi-purchase safety failures →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 2:
Review: "This mattress gets 4 stars for comfort but 5 overall for features"
<Reasoning>Explicit overall rating overrides component score →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 3:
Review: "Pyrex lids wore out but containers remain daily drivers"
<Reasoning>Core functionality maintains satisfaction despite part replacement →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 4:
Review: "Requires breaking in but then works perfectly"
<Reasoning>Initial effort needed reduces rating →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

Example 5:
Review: "Matches premium brand for daily tasks"
<Reasoning>Brand parity in practical use →5</Reasoning>
<Rating>5</Rating>

Example 6:
Review: "Good for light use but material feels cheap"
<Reasoning>Clear compromise reduces rating →3</Reasoning>
<Rating>3</Rating>

Example 7:
Review: "Weekly adjustments needed for basic function"
<Reasoning>Persistent maintenance →2</Reasoning>
<Rating>2</Rating>

Example 8:
Review: "UPDATE: Failed after 3mo. Do not recommend"
<Reasoning>Negative update negates initial satisfaction →1</Reasoning>
<Rating>1</Rating>

Example 9:
Review: "Perfect color match but average performance"
<Reasoning>Aesthetic praise without functional enthusiasm →4</Reasoning>
<Rating>4</Rating>

</FewShotExamples>

<Task>
Review: {review}
Rating:
</Task>
# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
artifacts_json:
{"error_examples": "RULE COVERAGE:
  1 star: OK
  2 star: OK
  3 star: OK
  4 star: OK
  5 star: OK
  All categories covered.

ERROR DISTRIBUTION (30 total misclassified):
  gold 4 -> 5: 14
  gold 5 -> 4: 7
  gold 1 -> 2: 3
  gold 5 -> 3: 1
  gold 1 -> 4: 1

MISCLASSIFIED EXAMPLES (your prompt got these wrong):
  1. Review: "We own a huge amount of Lodge cast iron cook and bake ware plus a few odd pieces collected over the years. Although I've tried other brands in the past, Lodge remained the best. Now, I'd like to say this was equal or better than Lodge but it's not......"
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 3 | Workers: [3, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  2. Review: "Wax and wicks. They work just fine."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 3] | Disagreement: 0.33
  3. Review: "While this looks cool, it functionally is no good.  To grate cheese, you have to hold onto the top of the taco.  Because it is plastic, the two sides squeeze together when gripped. This, coupled with the slipperiness of the plastic, makes this thing ..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [2, 1, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17
  4. Review: "Made in the USA.  It's the only candles I'll buy since I've just read too much about what can be in foreign made candles.  Things like lead. (ugh)

Normally I buy Yankee candles but I found this Village Candle to be just as nice if not nicer.  The sc..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  5. Review: "I like this towel. It is 100% Turkish cotton and measures 27 inches by 55 inches. The dobby weave is somewhat intricate so you are going to get some good absorbency. This towel also comes in a bath sheet, which is competively priced with the local st..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  6. Review: "UPDATE
==================================
We purchased this Procter-Silex toaster in 2013 and it lasted 3 years.  We were okay with that.

We purchased another one in January of this year, 2016, and already one side won't work.  Less than 2 months is..."
     Gold: 1 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  7. Review: "I think a toaster should last longer than 2 years.
We purchased this toaster in April 2016.  Last month a knob broke off.  Yesterday the right slot stopped working.

I don't know why it's impossible to make a good toaster anymore but this one makes b..."
     Gold: 3 | Predicted: 2 | Workers: [1, 2, 2] | Disagreement: 0.17

BORDERLINE EXAMPLES (correct but workers disagreed):
  1. Review: "It's a very solid bed that feels like it's going to hold up to just about anything. Assembly wasn't too difficult, though there were a couple of parts that had to be under some pressure to get the screw holes to line up just right. Also, when we firs..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [3, 4, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  2. Review: "My wife and I recently got talked into a camping weekend, and as neither of us has camped in decades, we wanted to do so in a bit of luxury.  I'm a rather large dude, and I've laid on enough beds that lost all of their air to know I wanted a good bed..."
     Gold: 5 | Predicted: 5 | Workers: [5, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17
  3. Review: "I like my Star Wars characters. I have a Darth Vader mug already and now I have the Chewbacca. The cup is nice and I like that it has the sash detail on the front and back so you know immediately it is Chewie. The mug can hold 20 ounces. As with most..."
     Gold: 4 | Predicted: 4 | Workers: [4, 5, 4] | Disagreement: 0.17

Summary: 30 errors, 18 borderline out of 80 examples."}
